message.content:
Okay! Imagine when you go to bed and the sun goes to bed, too, everything gets really, really quiet and dark. In the dark, it’s harder to see what’s around you, so your imagination starts to play games and you might think there are spooky things hiding in the shadows, even though they aren’t really there. 

During the day, the sun is out, everything is bright, and you can see everything around you clearly, so it feels safer. That’s why watching a scary movie at night feels scarier—the dark makes your imagination come up with more spooky things because it can't see what's really there!
